source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A student horizontally struck a small ball from the edge of a platform at a height of $h=0.8\mathrm{m}$ above the ground. The horizontal distance of the projectile motion when the ball landed was measured to be $s=1.2\mathrm{m}$. Neglecting air resistance, $g$ is taken as $10\mathrm{m}/\mathbf{s}^{2}$.

1. The time the small ball spends in the air
2. The horizontal initial velocity of the ball when it is struck.
3. The speed of the ball when it hits the ground.

image_dependency: All needed quantities (h=0.8 m, s=1.2 m, g=10 m/s^2) are stated in text; no diagram-dependent spatial reading is required
multi_step: The problem has three linked sub-questions: time in air, horizontal initial velocity, and impact speed
Solving requires at least multiple physics steps: compute fall time from height, compute horizontal speed from distance/time, then combine horizontal and vertical components for impact speed
The later sub-answers depend on earlier derived values rather than direct retrieval from a single formula
verifiability: The provided answers are explicit numeric results with units, which are easy to check against standard projectile-motion formulas
Each sub-answer can be independently verified from the given constants and conditions

decision_reasons: 题干与答案资产完整，question_present=true 且 answer_present=true，registry_passed=true，且无 issues
该题不依赖图片，image_dependency_score=0.0，所需物理量均已在文本中给出，核心语义完整可恢复
综合质量较高，multi_step_score=0.84 且 verifiability_score=0.96，属于可验证的多步物理解题样本
仅存在 multi_part_short_text_format_variation 风险，属于答案格式层面的轻微风险，不影响进入候选池
